ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for battered deep-fried squid wing on skewer; closest parent 'squid food product' used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers squids and cuttlefishes as a category; no specific code for battered fried squid wing skewer exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for this specific prepared squid snack product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one sotong wing on a stick, weighing approximately 40 g, as sold by Old Chang Kee at SGD 2.50 per piece.

nutrient_source.title: Fried squid (calamari) battered — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite; adapted for battered deep-fried squid wing
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo entry exists for Sotong Wing OnStik. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for battered deep-fried squid (calamari), cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy entry for fried squid (NDB 15151) and general battered fried seafood snack profiles. Energy and macros reflect the battered, deep-fried preparation. Sodium is estimated from typical seasoned battered fried squid products. Confidence is medium due to absence of an exact Singapore-specific entry.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of battered fried foods — analogous estimate based on battered fried fish/seafood products; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Sotong Wing OnStik. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous battered deep-fried seafood snacks with wheat flour coating. Wheat flour batter is the primary glycemic driver. Glycemic load per 40 g serving = (55 × 7.4 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 4.1. Available carbohydrate per serving = 18.5 g/100g × 40 g / 100 = 7.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~420 mg/100 g; ~168 mg per 40 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this deep-fried battered squid snack as one 40 g serving contributes approximately 168 mg sodium (~7% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), and frequent snacking on multiple pieces can accumulate meaningful sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~55) from wheat flour batter with moderate carbohydrate load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this snack in moderation; the wheat flour batter contributes a low GI of approximately 55 and a glycemic load of ~4 per 40 g serving, which is manageable within a controlled carbohydrate meal plan but should be accounted for in daily carbohydrate budgets.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation contributing total fat (~10.6 g/100 g) and cholesterol (~198 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake of this deep-fried squid snack due to its elevated total fat, saturated fat (~1.9 g/100 g), and cholesterol content (~198 mg/100 g), which can contribute to unfavourable lipid profiles when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sotong Wing OnStik is a popular Singaporean deep-fried seafood snack sold on a skewer, most famously associated with Old Chang Kee, a well-known local snack chain. 'Sotong' is the Malay and Singaporean colloquial word for squid or cuttlefish, and this product specifically uses the wing (fin) portion of the squid, which is coated in a seasoned wheat flour batter and deep-fried until golden and crispy. The result is a tender, slightly chewy squid interior encased in a light, crunchy batter shell, served hot on a bamboo stick for convenient on-the-go eating. The snack is priced at approximately SGD 2.50 per piece and is widely available at Old Chang Kee outlets across Singapore. Nutritionally, each 40 g piece provides approximately 92 kcal, 6.3 g protein, 4.2 g fat, and 7.4 g carbohydrate. The sodium content (~168 mg per serving) warrants attention for individuals managing hypertension. The low glycemic index (~55) from the wheat flour batter means individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should account for this snack within their daily carbohydrate allowance. The deep-fried preparation and moderate cholesterol content (~79 mg per serving) are relevant considerations for cardiovascular health. The snack is halal-certified and dairy-free but contains gluten (wheat) and molluscs (squid) as allergens. It is a culturally iconic street food snack in Singapore, often consumed as an afternoon snack or alongside other Old Chang Kee items such as curry puffs.

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for Sotong Wing OnStik. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for battered deep-fried squid (calamari), which is the closest approved-source match. GI is an analogous estimate based on battered fried seafood with wheat flour coating from the University of Sydney GI Database literature. Confidence is medium rather than low because the food type (battered deep-fried squid) is well-characterised in USDA data and the adaptation is defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods data for battered deep-fried squid (calamari); no exact Singapore-specific database entry was found in HPB Foodo for this product. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source data for this food category. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source data for this food category. 4. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Sotong Wing OnStik was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. Serving size of 40 g is estimated based on typical Old Chang Kee single-piece snack weight; exact weight may vary slightly by outlet. 6. Halal status is based on Old Chang Kee's publicly stated halal certification from MUIS (Islamic Religious Council of Singapore). 7. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as squid flesh and wheat flour batter contain negligible vitamin C; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central data for fried squid.
